qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Low-privilege project member can trigger server-side requests through webhook callbacks
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is a webhook SSRF in `webhooks/api.py` and `webhooks/utils.py`, while the advisory is a stored XSS in `templates/base.html` caused by rendering `user.custom_hotkeys` without escaping script-breaking characters.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory's vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is the `custom_hotkeys` stored-XSS path in `templates/base.html`, but the only qualifying finding is a separate webhook SSRF path.
evidence: The advisory describes stored XSS through `user.custom_hotkeys` rendered in `templates/base.html`, with payload injection via `PATCH /api/users/{id}` and token theft through `/api/current-user/token`.
The upstream fix commit `ea2462bf042bbf370b79445d02a205fbe547b505` changes `label_studio/templates/base.html` to escape `<` and `>` in `user.custom_hotkeys` before inserting it into the script block.

The only qualifying finding, `webhook_ssrf_low_priv`, instead targets `POST /api/webhooks/`, annotation-triggered webhook emission, and `requests.post(webhook.url, ...)` in `webhooks/utils.py`, which is a different source-to-sink path and a different vulnerability class.
